Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Understand the Models Goal Parse the Python code to understand the structure of model1 and model2

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: POWL Model 1 Analysis Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The edge Screen Decide and Screen Interview means Screen is a predecessor to both There is no edge between Decide and Interview So Decide and Interview are concurrent or at least one does not strictly precede the other

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a standard process you usually interview before making a decision Here Decide and Interview are concurrent You could make a decision without interviewing The lack of order between Decide and Interview is the key issue

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: POWL Model 2 Analysis loop onboarding means Onboard happens at least once It can happen multiple times xor payroll means either Payroll happens or skip happens This means Payroll is optional

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Post splits to Screen and Interview This means Screen and Interview are concurrent Interview leads to Decide Decide leads to loop onboarding loop onboarding leads to xor payroll xor payroll leads to Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Screen and Interview are concurrent In many processes you screen before interviewing Screening usually filters candidates to select who gets interviewed If they are concurrent this is a deviation Screen should likely precede Interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payroll is optional If skip is chosen the employee is onboarded but not added to payroll This is a severe anomaly This violates the core purpose of Hire to Retire where payroll is essential

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Compare Anomalies and Severity Model 1 anomalies Decide and Interview are concurrent Impact Decision might be made without interview data Interview might happen after decision This breaks the causal link between evidence interview and decision Severity High

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 anomalies Screen and Interview are concurrent Impact Interviewing without screening risks wasting resources on unqualified candidates Severity Medium Payroll is optional Impact Employee might not be paid This is a fundamental violation of the employment contract and the process goal Severity Critical

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close Model 1 Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close with Decide and Interview parallel Model 2 Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close with Screen and Interview parallel and Payroll optional

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait Screen is not an ancestor of Decide in Model 2 So Decide can happen without Screen This is a huge anomaly You can decide to hire someone without screening them

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 requires Screening before Decision Screen Decide Model 2 does not require Screening before Decision and also makes Payroll optional

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 is fundamentally broken No payroll is a showstopper Model 1 is flawed but functional You can hire someone without an interview but the process completes with payroll Model 1 is closer to normative

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Did I interpret xor Payroll skip correctly Yes XOR means one path is taken If skip is taken Payroll is not executed Payroll is optional

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 lacks Interview Decide This is the flaw Model 2 has Interview Decide but Screen is not before Decide So Decide can happen without Screen Result stands Model 2 is worse
